title: Search in BST

starterCode.python:
# Definition for a  binary tree node
# class TreeNode:
#    def __init__(self, x):
#        self.val = x
#        self.left = None
#        self.right = None

class Solution:
    # @param A : root node of tree
    # @param B : integer
    # @return an integer
    def solve(self, A, B):

hints: Use the BST property: left subtree < root < right subtree.
Start from the root and compare the value with B.
If current node value == B, return 1 (found).
If current node value < B, search in the right subtree.
If current node value > B, search in the left subtree.
If you reach null, return 0 (not found).
You can use iterative or recursive approach.
